name: clawsig-framework-vnext
description: Broaden Clawsig into a policy-bound, attested, marketplace-enforced assurance framework after Privacy Assurance v1.

userStories.title: Repair clawverify-cli submit/test debt
userStories.description: Fix the existing work-submit parser/transform debt so the canonical clawverify-cli test suite is structurally runnable again.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/clawverify-cli/test/work-submit.test.ts parses and runs in CI
No duplicate declarations / truncated file syntax remains
Regression coverage preserves current submit-path behavior
Release/test guardrails fail closed when the suite regresses again

userStories.title: Deterministic SIGTERM proof finalization
userStories.description: Make wrap shutdown/finalization deterministic so proof bundles are still emitted when the parent or child exits under SIGTERM scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: wrap-sigterm tests are green
Proof bundle emission survives child-normal-exit and parent-SIGTERM paths
Run summary / bundle writing is atomic enough to avoid partial artifacts
No regression in normal wrap success/failure reporting

userStories.title: Canonical receipt-only proof bundles
userStories.description: Remove unsigned synthetic/fallback receipt leakage from canonical proof-bundle paths so offline verification only sees verifier-supported evidence shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical proof bundles do not mix unsigned synthetic observations into payload.receipts
Fallback/internal observations live in explicit non-canonical metadata channels or are omitted
Offline verify/prove/export paths agree on what counts as canonical evidence
Regression tests cover the previously confusing fallback/genealogy cases

userStories.title: Release guardrail for green proof-production paths
userStories.description: Close the loop on framework reliability with CI/release guardrails that require canonical proof-production paths to stay green before release.
acceptanceCriteria: Guardrail workflow fails when submit/wrap/prove critical paths regress
Guardrail covers at least work-submit, wrap-sigterm, and proof-bundle compatibility
Failure output points directly to broken proof-production surfaces

userStories.title: Signed policy bundle schema + envelope
userStories.description: Define canonical signed policy bundles so assurance policy stops living as ambient runtime config and becomes a first-class verifiable artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical policy bundle payload + envelope schemas exist
Policy bundle carries policy hash, scope, issuer, issued_at, and additive policy fields
Verifier/CLI can parse and validate signed policy bundles

userStories.title: Hierarchical policy resolution
userStories.description: Resolve assurance policy deterministically across org/project/task/exception scopes and bind the effective policy into proofed runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Effective policy resolution order is deterministic and documented
Project/task exceptions are explicit and auditable
Proofed runs materialize a single effective policy snapshot/hash
Regression tests cover conflict/override behavior fail-closed

userStories.title: Signed approval receipts
userStories.description: Replace ad hoc approval tokens with signed approval receipts so exception paths are auditable and verifier-usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Approval is represented as a signed receipt, not a raw shared-secret token
Receipts bind approver identity, scope, policy hash, expiry, and decision
Require-approval flows fail closed without a valid signed approval receipt

userStories.title: Policy-hash-bound verification
userStories.description: Bind proof receipts and verifier outcomes to the canonical effective policy hash so claims are about a signed policy, not ambient env state.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifier checks effective policy hash where policy-bound receipts require it
prove/export surfaces the active policy hash/profile coherently
Policy mismatches fail closed with deterministic reason codes

userStories.title: Runner measurement manifest
userStories.description: Establish a measurable runner identity surface (binary/runtime manifest, loaded policy/runtime fingerprint) that later attestations can sign.
acceptanceCriteria: Runner measurement manifest captures binary/runtime/material inputs deterministically
Manifest is stable enough to hash/sign
Manifest generation works in proofed runs without breaking existing receipts

userStories.title: Signed runner attestation receipt
userStories.description: Emit a signed attestation receipt that binds runner measurement, effective policy hash, and key binary/runtime hashes to the run.
acceptanceCriteria: Attestation receipt binds measurement manifest + effective policy hash
Verifier validates signature and binding fail-closed
Receipt schema and tests cover malformed/forged attestation paths

userStories.title: Attested trust-tier uplift
userStories.description: Teach the verifier and framework surfaces to distinguish attested runs from merely observed runs, with explicit trust-tier uplift rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifier outputs an attested assurance tier when attestation is valid
Invalid/missing attestation does not accidentally uplift trust
Reason codes explain why an attested tier was or was not granted

userStories.title: Attestation posture in prove/export
userStories.description: Surface attestation state coherently in prove/export/reporting so reviewers can see whether the runner itself was measured and policy-bound.
acceptanceCriteria: prove/export report attested vs non-attested posture clearly
What-is-proven / not-proven boundaries mention runner attestation honestly
HTML/export pack include attestation posture when present

userStories.title: Bounty-level assurance requirements
userStories.description: Let marketplaces/bounties require specific assurance/privacy posture levels, processors, and approval policies before work is accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: Bounty/task contract can declare required assurance policy/tier
Requirements are machine-readable and shown to workers before submission
Unsupported requirement sets fail closed rather than silently downgrade

userStories.title: Submission gating on required evidence
userStories.description: Reject or quarantine submissions that do not satisfy bounty-required evidence/tiers instead of relying on manual reviewer memory.
acceptanceCriteria: Submission path enforces required assurance/privacy evidence
Auto-rejection/quarantine reason codes are deterministic
Reviewer UI can see exactly which required evidence was missing/invalid

userStories.title: Payout and review policy by assurance tier
userStories.description: Use assurance tiers and approval evidence to drive review routing, payout readiness, and escalation rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Marketplace can route/pause payouts based on assurance tier and approval evidence
Reviewer workflows expose why a payout is gated
Regression tests cover both satisfied and blocked payout paths

userStories.title: Hosted reviewer/export-pack viewer
userStories.description: Turn export packs into a portable hosted reviewer surface so buyers/auditors can inspect runs without raw repo/file spelunking.
acceptanceCriteria: Export pack can be rendered in a portable hosted/local viewer
Viewer aligns with prove/export language and privacy posture sections
Viewer supports direct artifact navigation without leaking creator-machine paths

userStories.title: Side-by-side run comparison
userStories.description: Allow reviewers to compare two runs and focus on privacy/assurance differences rather than manually diffing JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Comparison view highlights evidence/tier/policy/processor/privacy deltas
Blocked attempts, approvals, and redactions are diffed coherently
Comparison artifacts can be exported/shared

userStories.title: Reviewer signoff receipt + dispute-note binding
userStories.description: Bind reviewer signoff and dispute notes into signed receipts so review outcomes themselves become auditable artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewer signoff receipt captures reviewer identity, decision, timestamp, and bound run/export pack
Dispute notes/evidence references are tied to that signoff
Verifier/viewer can surface signoff/dispute state

userStories.title: Richer sensitivity taxonomy + custom rules
userStories.description: Expand sensitivity handling beyond the minimal v1 classes with org-defined patterns and a more usable class taxonomy.
acceptanceCriteria: Policy supports richer built-in classes and custom rule definitions
Rule/class IDs remain deterministic and signed-receipt compatible
False-positive/false-negative paths are tested explicitly

userStories.title: Structured redaction + simulation mode
userStories.description: Support structured redaction and a simulation/preview mode so teams can tune policy without flying blind.
acceptanceCriteria: Structured fields can be redacted deterministically
Simulation mode reports what would have been blocked/redacted without allowing silent bypass in proofed mode
Receipts distinguish simulated vs enforced actions clearly

userStories.title: Python proofed adapter parity
userStories.description: Bring the proofed policy/receipt/attestation surface to Python agent runs so the framework is not Node-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Python proofed adapter routes through the same assurance policy model
Core receipts/evidence surfaces remain verifier-compatible
At least one end-to-end Python proofed path is tested

userStories.title: Browser/automation proofed adapter parity
userStories.description: Extend assurance controls to browser/automation runners so reviewer trust does not depend on a single runtime family.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser automation runner can emit compatible assurance/privacy evidence
Policy and attestation surfaces remain coherent across runtimes
At least one browser-run proof path is tested end-to-end

userStories.title: Transparency log for assurance receipts
userStories.description: Add transparency-log style anchoring for policy, attestation, and reviewer receipts so retroactive tampering is harder to hide.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected assurance receipts can be anchored in a transparency log
Verifier can check inclusion/consistency evidence when present
Log integration remains optional/additive for non-logged runs

userStories.title: Revocation for runner/policy/reviewer keys
userStories.description: Provide revocation semantics for compromised or retired runner, policy-issuer, and reviewer keys so old trust roots can be withdrawn cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Revocation artifacts exist for runner/policy/reviewer identities
Verifier rejects revoked trust roots deterministically
Review/export surfaces explain revocation-driven failures

metadata.status: 24 planned stories across reliability, policy, attestation, marketplace, review UX, DLP, runtime parity, and transparency.
